曼谷（路透社）-泰国的Mana Srivate在26岁的救援人员中进行了数十次复苏尝试，但从未尝试过大象。
  在周日晚间出差时，Mana被采取行动，在东部省府尖竹汶（Chanthaburi）与一群野生厚皮动物过马路时，成功地恢复了骑摩托车撞击的小象。
 在周一在泰国社交媒体上传播开来的一段视频中，人们看到了Mana对两只小象的两只手进行压缩，因为几米外的同事在地板上对待昏迷而受伤的摩托车手。
   玛娜在电话中告诉路透社：“挽救生命是我的本能，但我一直担心，因为我能听到母亲和其他大象在呼唤婴儿。”
  他说：“根据人类理论和我在网上看到的视频片段，我假设会在哪里放置大象的心脏。”
   大象在大约10分钟后站起来，被送往另一个地方接受治疗，然后返回事故现场，希望与母亲团聚。
  尽管已经处理了数十起涉及人类的道路交通事故，Mana说这只大象是他在进行心肺复苏（CPR）时设法恢复生命的唯一受害者。

BANGKOK (Reuters) - Thailand’s Mana Srivate has performed dozens of resuscitation attempts in his 26 years as a rescue worker, but never before on an elephant.
  Mana was called into action while off duty on a road trip late on Sunday, successfully reviving a baby elephant struck by a motorcycle while crossing a road with a group of wild pachyderms in the eastern province of Chanthaburi.
 In a video that went viral on social media in Thailand on Monday, Mana is seen giving two-handed compressions to a small elephant lying on its side as colleagues a few metres away treat a dazed and injured motorcycle rider on the floor.
   “It’s my instinct to save lives, but I was worried the whole time because I can hear the mother and other elephants calling for the baby,” Mana told Reuters by phone.
  “I assumed where an elephant heart would be located based on human theory and a video clip I saw online,” he said.
   The elephant stood up after about 10 minutes and was taken to another location for treatment, before being returned to the scene of the accident in the hope of being reunited with its mother.
  Despite having dealt with dozens of road traffic accidents involving humans, Mana said the elephant was the only victim he had managed to revive while performing Cardiopulmonary resuscitation (CPR).
title: 骑摩托车击中的泰国小象在接受心肺复苏后幸存
title_en: Thai baby elephant hit by motorcycle survives after receiving CPR

谷歌已宣布自己是“业内最清洁的云”，原因是它仅使用可再生能源，并宣布了沙特阿拉伯一个新的云区域，该区域将由国有油气公司沙特阿美（Saudi Aramco）转售。
    该公告还透露，谷歌将在智利和德国建立云区域。这三个新区域都将具有三个可用区，以增强弹性（至少在Google用软件更新破坏它们之前）。智利将是Google继圣保罗之后的第二个地区，但谷歌并未透露将在智利哪个城市容纳该设施。与Google目前所说的新地区“将补充我们现有的法兰克福地区”相比，德国用户还必须等待更多细节。
  沙特人也没有被告知未来的Google云数据中心的位置。但谷歌透露，这项新投资是与阿美公司签署的2018年谅解备忘录“共同探索在该地区建立云服务”的结果。
  Google透露了版本控制功能，而且不期望零值会导致Gmail休假不便
  阅读更多
  “在该谅解备忘录的基础上，我们于2020年12月达成了一项协议，Google Cloud现在将在沙特阿拉伯部署和运营一个Cloud区域，而由Aramco赞助的本地战略经销商将为客户提供云服务，尤其侧重于王国中的企业。”
  在王储穆罕默德·本·萨勒曼（Mohammed bin Salman）在2018年访问Google之后，谷歌/沙特阿拉伯之间的关系蓬勃发展，产生了上述谅解备忘录。 
王储已经改革了该国对妇女权利的一些限制，降低了宗教警察的影响力，并试图使化石燃料以外的国家经济多样化。  他还清除了竞争对手，鼓励在也门进行代理战争，并对不逊色于媒体的报道充满敌意。 当然，他被怀疑知道记者Jamal Khashoggi被谋杀的消息。JamalKhashoggi被肢解在沙特阿拉伯的伊斯坦布尔领事馆。  在那些抗议该公司为美军工作的谷歌员工中，或者在AI研究人员蒂姆尼特·格布鲁（Timnit Gebru）突然而奇怪的离职之后，这种情况如何下降还有待观察。 与全球最大的化石燃料公司的分支机构合作的内部反应也是如此。 ®  注册-技术社区的独立新闻和观点。 情境发布的一部分
note_en:
Google has proclaimed itself the “cleanest cloud in the industry” due to its exclusive use of renewable energy and announced a new cloud region in Saudi Arabia that will be resold by an arm of state-owned oil and gas company Saudi Aramco.
    The announcement also revealed that Google will build cloud regions in Chile and Germany. All three new regions will have three availability zones to enhance resilience (at least until Google  breaks them with a software update). Chile will be Google’s second region after Sao Paolo, but Google hasn’t said which Chilean city will house the facility. German users must also wait for more detail than Google’s current statement that the new region “will complement our existing region in Frankfurt.”
  Saudis also haven’t been told the location of their future Google cloud data centers. But Google has revealed that the new investment came about as a result of a 2018 memorandum of understanding with Aramco “to jointly explore establishing cloud services in the region.”
  Google reveals version control plus not expecting zero as a value caused Gmail to take an inconvenient early holiday
  READ MORE
  “Building on that MoU, we concluded an agreement in December 2020 and Google Cloud will now deploy and operate a Cloud region in Saudi Arabia, while a local strategic reseller, sponsored by Aramco, will offer cloud services to customers, with a particular focus on businesses in the Kingdom.”
  The Google/Saudi Arabia relationship flourished after crown prince Mohammed bin Salman visited Google in 2018, resulting in the abovementioned memorandum of understanding.
  The crown prince has reformed some of his nation’s restrictions on women’s rights, decreased the influence of religious police and tried to diversify the nation’s economy beyond fossil fuels.
  He’s also purged rivals, encouraged a proxy war in Yemen and been hostile to less-than-fawning media coverage. And of course, he is suspected of being aware of the murder of journalist Jamal Khashoggi, who was dismembered in Saudi Arabia’s Istanbul consulate.
    Just how that goes down among those Googlers who have protested the company’s work for the US military, or the  sudden and odd departure of AI researcher Timnit Gebru remains to be seen. As does the internal reaction to working with an arm of the world’s largest fossil fuel company. ®

title: Google说这是最干净的云。 与沙特石油签署新地区协议

本文是我们正在进行的有关巴基斯坦技术禁令的最新系列。了解该国对Tinder和TikTok的禁令。
 阿迪·扎曼·汗（Aadee Zaman Khan）是一名职业医生，但内心却是名人。正如在线粉丝们所知，Zee博士定期在Bigo（中国直播应用程序）上直播，Bigo在巴基斯坦受到追捧。在他的直播中，他经常沿着白沙瓦（Peshawar）的主要道路行驶，在路边加油站与英俊的军人调情，有时还假装成想象中的狗仔队。他柔和，男孩气的外表掩盖了他的网络个性。他以口头上的优势吸引了大部分男性观众。他们喜欢它。
 直到七月，巴基斯坦当局才突然禁止比戈。该国的电信管理局宣布了禁令，不到一个月后，邻国印度就在北部边界与中国军队发生冲突，从网上商店中删除了数十种中国移动应用程序。 Bigo是巴基斯坦今年禁止使用的众多应用程序中的第一个，其中包括TikTok。尽管TikTok禁令得到了广泛报道，但Bigo所发生的事情几乎没有在国外注册。
 Bigo是中国科技巨头JOYY Inc.的子公司，自2016年以来一直在南亚和东南亚开展业务。该应用程序允许客户按地理位置访问其平台上的任何直播：其界面按国家和城市显示直播，因此巴基斯坦可以像在越南一样轻松地在喀布尔找到并观看直播。它的4亿用户主要集中在全球南部，例如印度，巴西，阿拉伯联合酋长国，孟加拉国，沙特阿拉伯和巴基斯坦。在亚洲，Bigo通常预装在相对便宜的中国智能手机上，而现在这些智能手机已经在大多数市场上普及了。
     在正式从巴基斯坦的应用程序商店中删除之前，Bigo在该国的工人阶级中大受欢迎。该公司对边缘化，代表性不足和新上线的社区的关注是有利可图的，有时甚至是掠夺性的。尽管有禁令，尽管Bigo仍然以有限的功能运行，但该应用程序已将一类新的消费者带到了网上，为未来的社交媒体平台所用。
 Bigo的算法鼓励完全陌生人按地理位置进行视频互动。但这不只是一个简单的直播应用程序：它是一款社交游戏，拥有自己的货币和规则的宇宙。用户可以玩游戏，在社交场合中排名上升，向陌生人添加实时视频通话，实时阅读消息，唱歌，制作短视频，以及通过这些互动来对抗他们的物理世界的分裂。
 对于Zee博士这样的用户，当该应用开始在巴基斯坦获得关注时，体验就得到了解放。在一次Bigo通话中，Zee博士告诉我，他在经历了“剧烈的性格改变”后偶然发现了Bigo。他告诉我：“我以来电者身份加入了两个星期，就像打电话给我一样。” “过了一会儿，我上线了，两天内，我有数百名观众。在三天之内，我变得病毒式传播。” 
他说，他不依赖Bigo来赚钱，就像应用程序上的其他应用程序一样，拥有大量追随者，尽管公司会在每15天达到40或更多小时的直播时间配额时才付钱给他，这通常是流行的彩带。对于Zee博士而言，这与金钱无关，它与培养粉丝群有关。 “我什至没有那么出名，但是当我在大街上外出时，人们会认出我！”他告诉我。人们希望看到新面孔。他们不想看到同样的老星星。他们想看到一个真实的人。”
 Bigo早期的直播非常真实。其中包括Bal路支（Baloch）和普什图（Pashtun）突袭者，他们沿着艰苦而往往致命的人口贩运路线中途停留，该路线将巴基斯坦人带到伊朗和土耳其，并带到了欧洲。用户在区域之间滑动。在海湾地区，您可以参加沙特阿拉伯的普什图（Pashtun）劳工，在经过犹太祈祷之后，播送他们的饭菜。在西班牙，萨拉基奇（Saraiki）一家店主向寻求离开该国之路的巴基斯坦观众提供签证和庇护小费；在纽约市，一名克什米尔人的出租车司机在家乡与妇女调情，向她们展示了他那天赚的钱。
 第一次来到巴基斯坦时打开应用程序，目睹这些场面对我来说很激动。突然之间，我可以与全国各地的人们互动-在现实世界中看不到的人们。巴基斯坦的官方语言乌尔都语仅占该国人口的7％，但当地方言很少出现在主流媒体中。在Bigo上，我可以和现场直播者说“普什图语”和“旁遮普语”，并观看其他方言之间的无缝切换。
 我看着我小时候在拉合尔长大的那种人，只有他们在公开场合表达自己。从他们的笑话到他们的外表，我看到了我自己。它正在验证。但是我也感到对空间的保护。即使该应用程序在工人阶级的巴基斯坦人中大受欢迎，但我知道，上层阶级不可避免地将其高档化会吸引国家的审查。从应用程序进入该国到2018年左右，巴基斯坦政府的严格审查一直没有改变狭窄的在线表达方式。我开始存档这些在线互动，记录并拍摄屏幕截图，以保存在Bigo上看到的短暂时刻。
     在该应用发布后的头几个月，即2016年3月，该应用程序最受欢迎的巴基斯坦广播公司是khwaja sira和生活在主要城市之外的工人阶级的酷儿们。溪流通常来自他们的卧室，谨慎的舞会，实际上有数千人参加，取决于帐户的受欢迎程度。比戈不仅为彩带提供安全保障，还比他们在现实世界中经常遇到的暴力局势更为安全-将它们与居住在国外的顾客联系起来。幸运的顾客甚至可以安排私人电话。
 应用内货币可能会以大多数当地货币兑现，这为用户提供了诱人的机会，让他们可以在Bigo上相互竞争。这是该应用程序如此令人上瘾的部分原因：观看直播时，鼓励您通过购买彩带礼物来参与其中，彩带可能会通过歌曲，舞蹈或什至有机会直接在直播中与他们交谈来奖励您。
 但是交换并不容易，而且Bigo所赚的不仅仅是处理费。在比戈（Bigo）的一些khwaja sira圈子中，有一个故事讲述了Meena，她是十几岁的来自Multan的khwaja sira女孩，她从迪拜的一家巴基斯坦承包商那里收到了价值5,000美元的电子礼品。他们说，这个人告诉梅埃纳（Meena），他已经卖掉了家人在旁遮普邦（Punjab）的农业用地，以吸引她的现场直播。在Bigo将这项交易削减了73％（包括30％的Android / iOS平台费用）后，Meena剩下了$ 1,000的现金。 
Meena的电子礼物可能在频谱的高端，但是像她这样的互动每天都在发生。当我登录Bigo时，我经常看到纽约市的一名出租车司机Zahid向白沙瓦的一位年轻歌手发送电子礼物，要求她唱他最喜欢的Pashto歌曲，因为他漫无目的地在顾客之间四处逛逛。电子礼物和应用内交易看起来像卡通漫画-货币被称为“豆子”和“钻石”，礼物可以包括亲吻表情符号，魔术蛋，雪花和数字花朵-但购买量会增加。 JOYY Inc.仅在2019年就获得了10亿美元的利润。它的许多用户每天的收入甚至不到1美元。
 该应用程序使用本地化的英语来访问各种各样的南亚消费者。新的彩带一经注册，便会显示“嗨，亲爱的，快来观看我的广播”和“你为什么迟到？”之类的通知。开始在屏幕上弹出。机器人是新直播者的鬼魂用户，可以真正体验一下。该应用程序的催眠吸引力难以抗拒：对于局限于农村和偏远地区的用户而言，与国外陌生人进行无尽互动的诱惑力很强。
 相同的漫游器会巡视直播，以执行Bigo不断变化的审查规则。尽管各地大致相同-禁止在直播中抽烟，因为乳沟过多，但解释的严重程度因地方政府法规而异。如果认为规则被违反，则Bigo的处罚很快：永久冻结帐户，包括所有电子货币。
 卡基·帕塔基（Kaki Pataki）或炸药婴儿，是一个易爆的，满嘴的khwaja sira女孩，曾经从巴基斯坦一个未公开的地点流过。她只挥舞着手脚，她经常讨论禁忌话题，例如男性阳ot和性幻想，从而吸引了成千上万的观众，从十几岁的男孩到老主妇，每个人都如此。 Kaki的名字成为叛逆的“性感女孩”角色的代名词。她的真实账户在2019年被禁止使用，据我认识她的流媒体说，她的大量电子货币被冻结了。 Kaki的帐户如此受欢迎，以至于随后出现了几个叫Kaki Pataki的模仿帐户；它已成为Bigo的品牌名称。
 Bigo的财务陷阱并没有阻止其在巴基斯坦的迅速崛起。 B名人和前板球明星在推出后的三年内便开始以Bigo身份出现在Bigo上。该应用程序的高档化已经开始。在巴基斯坦，随着主流用户的到来，政府也在进行审查。这个国家有禁令的历史，但是对社交媒体平台，电影和电视节目的频繁审查已经训练了公众，使其有足够的资源去寻找其他方式来追逐他们的欲望。
 “禁令在这里无关紧要，” Zee博士不分阶段地告诉我。他说，Bigo仍在巴基斯坦最大的4G网络之一Zong上运行，这很可能是因为它属于中国电信巨头中国移动。 VPN还使访问该应用程序变得容易。一份报告称，即使对比戈的禁令生效后，巴基斯坦板球队的成员仍继续从直播中牟利。 Zee博士说：“ [审查]是因为该应用程序出名而进行的。” “现在每个人都知道Bigo是什么。”
    在禁止Bigo之后的几个月中，审查机构还禁止了约会应用程序，例如Tinder和Grindr，以及简短的TikTok。只有在TikTok表示将审查涉及传播“淫秽和不道德行为”的帐户之后，才取消对后者的禁令。总体而言，禁令为巴基斯坦的检查机构指明了新的重点。这些应用所代表的简短的自由表达窗口现在将像该国所有其他媒体一样被巡逻。 
由于没有Bigo，Bigo最受欢迎的帐户持有人正在将他们新发现的社交媒体技能带入Instagram和Facebook等主流平台。 Zee博士在Facebook，YouTube和Instagram上拥有超过半百万的追随者。许多大型节目主持人，包括Zee博士，都向观众提供幸运抽奖礼物，例如免费iPhone甚至是参观麦加的门票。 “比戈是我保持名望的地方，” Zee博士告诉我。 “但与此同时，比戈已成为我的家人。”
 像Bigo和TikTok这样的应用程序使被忽视的下层阶级可以通过口述传统，表演和短片来存档自己的历史，文化和亚文化。他们感到被别人看到和听到的信心，就像他们一样。在巴基斯坦，比戈使边缘化群体能够以前所未有的方式自由发言。该应用程序为他们提供了一种在线社交资本的形式，也可以转化为离线世界。
 Zee博士在Bigo上名列前茅-如此之多，以至于他去年在该应用程序在该国举行的首届颁奖典礼上获得了荣誉-他说Bigo为他提供了另一种成名的途径。他告诉我：“我们不允许人们成为这个国家的明星。” “我们阻止星星发芽。”他说他的狂热使他有责任感。
 在我们谈话结束时（数百人正在观看他的直播），他说：“关于Bigo的最好的事情是，即使在各大洲，两个聪明的人也可以互相交谈。”不久之后，他接了另一个直播人的电话。
  我们每周的时事通讯使您可以首先访问我们的最新新闻，趋势发现以及来自世界各地的技术分析。立即注册。
note_en:
This article is the latest in our ongoing series about technology bans in Pakistan. Read about the country’s ban on  Tinder and  TikTok.
 Aadee Zaman Khan is a doctor by profession but a celebrity at heart. Dr. Zee, as he’s known to his fans online, goes live regularly on Bigo, a Chinese livestreaming app with a cult following in Pakistan. In his livestreams, he’s often speeding down Peshawar’s main road, flirting with handsome servicemen at roadside gas stations, sometimes pretending to be tailed by imaginary paparazzi. His soft, boyish looks belie his online personality; he’s known for verbally dominating his mostly male viewers. They love it.
 That was, until July, when Pakistani authorities abruptly banned Bigo. The country’s telecoms authority  announced the ban less than a month after neighboring India  removed dozens of Chinese mobile apps from online stores following skirmishes with Chinese troops on its northern border. Bigo was the first of many apps to be banned by Pakistan this year, including, briefly, TikTok. While the TikTok ban  was covered widely, what happened to Bigo barely registered outside of the country.
 A subsidiary of Chinese tech giant  JOYY Inc., Bigo has been operating in South and Southeast Asia  since 2016. The app allows customers to tap into any livestream on its platform by geography: Its interface shows livestreams by country and city, so a user in Pakistan could find and watch a livestream in Kabul just as easily as they could one in Vietnam. Its  400 million users are mostly concentrated in the global south, in countries such as India, Brazil, the United Arab Emirates, Bangladesh, Saudi Arabia, and Pakistan. In Asia, Bigo often comes pre-installed on the relatively cheap Chinese smartphones now ubiquitous in most markets.
     Before it was officially removed from Pakistan’s app stores, Bigo had been a runaway hit with the country’s working class. The company’s focus on a marginal, underrepresented, and newly online community has been lucrative — at times, even financially predatory. And while Bigo still functions in a limited capacity in spite of the ban, the app has brought a new class of consumers online, one primed for the future social media platforms that will take its place.
 Bigo’s algorithm encourages video interactions between complete strangers by geography. But it’s more than just a simple livestreaming app:  It’s a social game, a universe with its own currency and rules. Users can play games, rise through social ranks, add strangers to live video calls, read messages in real time, sing, make short videos, and, through these interactions, defy the divisions of their physical world.
 For users like Dr. Zee, when the app started gaining traction in Pakistan, the experience was liberating. On a Bigo call, Dr. Zee tells me he stumbled onto Bigo after some “drastic personality changes.” “I joined for two weeks just as a caller, the way you’re calling me,” he told me. “A little bit later, I went live, and within two days, I had hundreds of viewers. Within three days, I went viral.”
 He said he doesn’t depend on Bigo for an income, as many others on the app with large followings do, although the company does pay him whenever he meets its quota of 40 or more hours of livestreaming per 15 days, a typical setup for popular streamers. For Dr. Zee, it’s not about the money: It’s about cultivating a fan base. “I’m not even that famous, but when I go out on the street, people recognize me!” he told me. “People want to see new faces. They don’t want to see the same old stars. They want to see someone real.”
 And the early Bigo livestreams were very real; they included Baloch and Pashtun asylees on stopovers along the arduous and often deadly human-trafficking route that brings Pakistanis through Iran and Turkey to Europe. Users flicked among regions. In the Gulf, you could join Pashtun laborers in Saudi Arabia broadcasting their meal preparations after jummah prayers; in Spain, a Saraiki shopkeeper giving visa and asylum tips to viewers in Pakistan seeking a way out of the country; in New York City, a Kashmiri cabdriver flirting with women in their home villages, showing them the dollars he had earned that day.
 Opening the app when it first came to Pakistan and witnessing these scenes was thrilling for me. Suddenly, I could interact with people all over the country — people I couldn’t see in my physical world. Urdu, the official language of Pakistan, is spoken natively by only 7% of the country’s population, but local dialects rarely appear in mainstream media. On Bigo, I could speak Pashto and Punjabi with livestreamers and watch others switch seamlessly among dialects.
 I watched the kind of people  I grew up around as a child in Lahore, only they were expressing themselves publicly. I saw myself in them, from their jokes to their appearances; it was validating. But I also felt protective of the space. Even though the app was overwhelmingly popular with working-class Pakistanis, I knew that its inevitable gentrification by the upper classes would attract state censorship. From the app’s arrival in the country until about 2018, a narrow window of online expression was untouched by the scrutiny of the Pakistani state. I began archiving these online interactions, recording and taking screenshots to preserve the ephemeral moments I saw on Bigo.
     In the first months after its launch, in March 2016, the app’s most popular Pakistani broadcasters were   khwaja sira and working-class queer people living outside of major cities. The streams would often be from their bedrooms, discreet dance parties virtually attended by thousands, depending on the account’s popularity. Bigo wasn’t just safer for the streamers  than the often violent situations they faced in the real world — it connected them with patrons living abroad. Lucky patrons could even arrange a private call.
 The in-app currency, which may be cashed out in most local currencies, presents an alluring opportunity for users to perform for one another on Bigo. It’s part of the reason the app is so addictive: Watching a livestream, you’re encouraged to participate by buying gifts for streamers, who might reward you with a song, a dance, or even an opportunity to speak with them directly on a livestream.
 But the exchange isn’t straightforward, and Bigo pockets a lot more than just the processing fee. In some of the   khwaja sira  circles on Bigo,  the story is told of Meena, a  khwaja sira girl from Multan in her late teens who received more than $5,000 worth of e-gifts from a Pakistani labor contractor in Dubai. They say  this man told Meena he had sold his family’s agricultural land back in Punjab to get her attention during her livestreams. After Bigo shaved 73% off this transaction — including a 30% Android/iOS  platform fee — Meena was left with $1,000 to cash out.
 Meena’s e-gifts might be on the higher end of the spectrum, but interactions like hers occur daily. When I would log on to Bigo, I frequently saw  Zahid, a cabdriver in New York City, sending electronic gifts to a young singer in Peshawar with requests that she sing his favorite Pashto songs, as he aimlessly drove around between customers. The e-gifts and in-app exchanges appear cartoonish — the currencies are called “beans” and “diamonds,” and gifts can include kiss emojis, magic eggs, snowflakes, and digital flowers — but the purchases add up. JOYY Inc. raked in $1 billion in profits in 2019 alone. Many of its users earn less than $1 a day, if at all.
 The app uses localized English to access a wide variety of South Asian consumers. As soon as a new streamer signs up, notifications like “Hi dear, come visit my broadcast” and “Why are you late?” start popping up on your screen. Bots appear as ghost users for new livestreamers to give a taste of a real audience. The app’s hypnotic appeal is tough to resist: For users limited to often rural and remote surroundings, the lure of endless interactions with strangers abroad is strong.
 The same bots patrol livestreams to enforce Bigo’s shifting censorship rules. While broadly the same everywhere — smoking a cigarette on a livestream is forbidden, as is revealing too much cleavage — the severity with which they are interpreted varies, depending on local government regulations. If the rules are deemed to have been broken, the punishment from Bigo is swift: a permanent account freeze, including all e-currency.
 Kaki Pataki, or Baby Dynamite, is an explosive, foulmouthed  khwaja sira girl who used to stream from an undisclosed location in Pakistan. She only streamed her hands and feet, and she regularly attracted thousands of viewers by discussing taboo subjects like male impotence and sexual fantasies, with everyone from teenage boys to old housewives. Kaki’s name became synonymous with a rebellious “sexy girl” persona. Her real account was banned in 2019, and according to streamers I spoke with who know her, her sizable e-currency holdings were frozen with it. Kaki’s account was so popular that several copycat accounts called Kaki Pataki have since popped up; it’s become a brand name on Bigo.
 Bigo’s financial traps didn’t deter its meteoric rise in Pakistan. B-list celebrities and former cricket stars began appearing on Bigo as official hosts within three years of its launch. The gentrification of the app had begun in earnest. And in Pakistan, with mainstream users comes government scrutiny. The country has a history of bans, but its frequent censorship of social media platforms, films, and TV shows has trained the public to be resourceful in finding alternative ways to chase their desires online.
 “Bans don’t matter here,” an unphased Dr. Zee told me. He said Bigo still works on one of Pakistan’s biggest 4G networks, Zong, likely because it’s owned by the Chinese telecoms giant China Mobile. VPNs have also made it easy to access the app; one report claims  members of the Pakistani cricket team continued to amass profits from livestreams even after the ban on Bigo went into effect.  “The [censorship] is happening as a result of the app getting famous,” said Dr. Zee. “Now everyone knows what Bigo is.”
    In the months following the ban on Bigo, censorship authorities  have also banned dating apps like Tinder and Grindr and, briefly,  TikTok. The ban on the latter was lifted only after TikTok said it would censor accounts involved in spreading  “obscenity and immorality on its own. Collectively, the bans indicate a new focus for Pakistan’s censorship authorities. The brief windows of free expression these apps represent are now to be patrolled just like all other media in the country.
 With its absence, Bigo’s most popular account holders are taking their newfound social media skills to mainstream platforms such as Instagram and Facebook. Dr. Zee has more than half a million followers on Facebook, YouTube, and Instagram combined. Many big streamers, including Dr. Zee, offer their viewers lucky-draw gifts, like free iPhones or even tickets to visit Mecca. “Bigo is a place for me to maintain my stardom,” Dr. Zee told me. “But at the same time, Bigo has become my family.”
 Apps like Bigo and TikTok have given the neglected underclass the ability to archive their own histories, cultures, and subcultures through oral tradition, performance, and short videos. They feel the confidence that comes with being seen and heard by others just like them. In Pakistan, Bigo enabled marginalized groups to speak freely like never before. The app gave them a form of online social capital that translates to the offline world too.
 Dr. Zee, who rose through the ranks on Bigo — so much so that he was honored at the app’s first award show in the country last year — said Bigo has given him an alternative path to fame. “We don’t let people become stars in this country,” he told me. “We stop stars from budding.” He said his fandom gives him a sense of responsibility.
 Toward the end of our conversation — one a few hundred were watching on his livestream — he said, “The best thing about Bigo is that two intelligent people, even across continents, can talk to each other.” Soon after, he took another livestreamer’s call.
  Our weekly newsletter offers you first access to our latest stories, trend-spotting, and analysis on tech from around the world. Sign up today.
title: 直播应用Bigo于7月在巴基斯坦被禁止使用，如何使该国被忽视的下层阶级和边缘化群体以前所未有的方式自由发表言论
title_en: How livestreaming app Bigo, banned in Pakistan in July, had enabled the country's neglected underclass and marginalized groups to speak freely like never before

这是我最喜欢的USDS故事之一：在USDS的职业生涯中，我很快就受到了声名狼藉，因为您可以向其发出SOS求救电话，而不仅仅是USDS内部，而是外部与承包商和其他政府合作伙伴的联系。政府甚至在公务员制度中都有相当多的背叛行为。每当您选择向第三方求助时，您都会冒与被求助者相处的危险。仅仅因为DC没有宫殿，并不意味着我们没有宫殿的阴谋。
 无论如何，有一天，这群安全研究人员与我联系，并让我知道与他们合作的该机构正受到国内白人至上主义者的积极攻击。他们已经警告了适当的人员并提出了建议，但是却四处奔波。白人至上主义者感兴趣的网站有成千上万个已知的安全漏洞，但它也有一个非常具体的发布日期，不可协商，并已写入法律（这在政府中非常普遍）。领导层不想停止开发工作来解决安全问题，但是也不能认为他们忽略了安全问题。尤其是其中的数千个。因此，他们将与研究人员互动，通过会议，实地考察和PowerPoint演示文稿进行这项大型表演，然后什么也不做。
 他们在多个方面都在这样做，外部顾问和政府监督部门的步伐都很缓慢。在这种情况下，我们无法向联邦政府负责信息安全的众多组织之一报告问题。每个可能被要求施加压力的人都已经意识到情况并施加最大压力。
 我建议我们让FBI知道，看看他们是否可以为我们阻止潜在的攻击者而无需担心网站。尚未犯下任何罪行，因此FBI在技术上无法对威胁采取任何措施。但是他们确实跟踪此类信息，也可能跟踪安全研究人员可以支持其他调查的内容。我花了几周的时间才找到合适的联系人并与我们联系。在确定他在相关工作组中并且他们确实跟踪了此信息并且对听到我们的发现感兴趣之后，我建议我们去喝咖啡并填写一些详细信息。我提供了一组潜在的位置，包括超好的代理商餐厅。
 “实际上您有可以使用的办公室或会议室吗？”
 当然，我说。这并没有打扰我，因为“让我们喝咖啡”一词总是很具象。首先，我不喝咖啡。
 我在USDS工作的很大一部分是喝咖啡。我们与官僚作风的方法是建立目标一致，技能或资源兼容的人际网络。与新节点的第一次接触始终是非正式的。我需要确定您的规模，确定您的动机，对风险的承受能力，如何定义成功。但是我也需要建立信任。我从未想过要以其他方式处理这种情况，仅仅是因为FBI处于另一端。 
无论如何，特工来到我的办公室，他关上门，拿出他的徽章。 “我必须首先向你展示。”
 这对我来说似乎很奇怪，因为我实际上没有任何理由相信他不是他所说的那个人，但是某些工作会使您产生偏执，所以我不予理会。
 当我们聊天时，我注意到他正在记很多非常详尽的笔记，这使我感到奇怪，但是-再次-我被解雇了。对话友好而富有成效。他适当地设定了期望。我们同意以后如何跟进。他站起来离开，握住我的手，转向门，但是当他碰到门把手时停了下来。
 “哦，等等，我忘记了一些东西。你能举起右手一分钟吗？”
  “就您所知，您今天告诉我的一切都是真实的吗？”
 我看着仍然举起的右手。哦……..“这是我不入狱一百万年的事情之一，对吧？”
    茫然无知，开始意识到我可能不小心咬了太多东西，最终我回到了USDS HQ，这是位于白宫外拉斐特公园旁的联排别墅。我在休息室碰到了马特·库茨（Matt Cutts）。 
“最奇怪的事情发生了，”我在告诉他整个故事之前说道。当我完成时，我意识到Matt只是凝视着我，完全保持沉默。他像这样盯着我，呆了很长时间。
      “我有200名员工，玛丽安。永远是你没有其他人陷入这些情况。永远是你如果有人告诉我，我的一名员工正在与FBI一起寻找白人至上主义者，那么我就不必再问是谁了，因为当然是您。永远是你为什么总是你呢？”
 人们倾向于认为职业生涯中最大的决定因素是他们的才华或技能，但我不相信。处理风险和处理恐惧的方式对您所从事的职业的影响比其他任何单一因素都大。在USDS，人们将我视为一个伟大的游戏猎人。少数几个人总是把真正重要的国家安全问题束之高阁，但是我没有从事那些项目，我只是抓住了办公桌旁碰到的机会，而其他人也不敢采取任何行动。在联邦调查局的情况下，我这样做的能力很大一部分是由于无知。我一直不停地认为联邦调查局的文化和过程可能会给我的外展活动赋予重要的意义，而其他组织则不会。但我也不认为自己承担着巨大的责任和所有相应的风险。我只是在那里连接两组人。为他们打开门，以便他们可以完成工作。我并不担心自己是否有资格或有能力处理国内白人至上主义者，因为我不认为这是我的职责。我相信研究人员擅长于他们的工作。我相信联邦调查局擅长他们的工作。
 促使人们表现出色的很多原因是他们的经理要期待他们的决策，而其中很多是要了解两件事：
  但这不仅仅是管理者考虑员工，在组织的任何级别生存下来都涉及预期他人的行为。当人们开始担当领导角色时，他们最终必须决定如何处理恐惧。当您是领导者时，您就是负责失败的人。您走得越高，您承担的责任就越多，对它的控制权也就越少。对团队的信任很重要，但是您对风险的感知和接受就更重要。如果您改变员工寻找值得信赖的员工，那么风险承受能力低的领导者就不会成为风险承受能力强的领导者。每个人都希望拥有这种隐含的，本能的，不需要思考并且永远不会怀疑的信任度。那是黄金标准。但是，大多数信任关系之所以形成，是因为您只是选择摒弃怀疑和信任，而不是因为您拥有这些强大的信仰储备。
 领导者在发展过程中倾向于在风险的两个方向之一上突破。有些领导人像武士一样拥抱死亡。他们将自己的角色视为保护和授权自己下面的人做真正的工作，即使个人情况不佳，也很荣幸能够为团队的利益服务。在最好的组织中，领导者的经理也不惧怕死亡，他们拥有与授予员工相同的安全性。
 但是有些人不会以这种方式处理风险。这些领导人被吸引尝试超越死亡，这在隐喻上和从字面上看都是不可能的。您无法避免永远承担责任。最终会有一些令人失望或错误的事情，您会被要求回答。 
风险承受能力低的领导者（我说领导者而不是经理，因为在工程团队中，这些概念也适用于员工和首席工程师）将通过完全避免风险或弄清楚如何扭转局势来应对风险。这不是他们的错（TM）
 这些领导人最终成为官僚。完美的工作是具有光荣头衔和高薪水的工作，无需做出任何决定，也无需冒险。这些领导者进入初创企业的频率比您预期的要高。那是因为继续从事您一直死里逃生的职业，会对您的心理造成真正的伤害。重要的是要理解，即使这些人似乎不在乎他们仍然是人类，他们仍然具有与其他任何人相同的基本需求和感觉。逃亡不仅伤害了他们丢在公共汽车下的人，还伤害了他们。他们希望得到员工的尊重，并且经常知道自己没有得到尊重。他们想受到钦佩，常常知道他们不是。他们也感到内ing地将人们束之高阁以求生存。
 当您处在受到尊重和钦佩的工作环境中时，这通常是它自己的奖励。如果没有的话，可以带给您价值感和满意度的工作要素都直接与职业阶梯相连。你叫什么名字您的项目的声望价值是多少？有多少人向您报告？有多少人向他们举报？您得到多少钱？你有多少股份？所有这一切变得非常重要，因为它通常就是您所拥有的。逃离死亡的人们处于持续的焦虑状态。这就像最糟糕的冒名顶替综合症。他们通过爬上职业阶梯来自我安抚。因此，即使您不想承担起责任，只想整天加盖橡皮戳，对于初创公司来说似乎不合适，但对于那些惧怕死亡的领导者来说，这些机会还是很有吸引力的，因为他们的威望可以代替他们做好工作的满意度。
 一旦人们开始以这种方式工作，就会变成一个不断升级的循环。没有人知道如何运营拥有1000名员工的工程组织。人们在准备晋升之前需要时间来培养技能。如果您的实际工作引起足够的恐惧和焦虑，使您背叛了最亲密的同事的信任，那么您就不会做得更好。获得的能力越远，压力越严重，逃脱死亡的速度就越快。
 最终，您最终将所有时间都花在试图显得忙碌，避免责任和与他人视线上来挽回面子。同时，不惧怕死亡的领导者往往会有较慢的职业发展，因为他们从实际工作中获得更多的满足感，并专注于培养可以使他们变得更好的技能。最终的结果是，通常感觉到每个高层都恶意地无能为力。
note_en:
This is one of my favorite USDS stories: Very quickly in my career at USDS I developed a repu  tation for being a person you could send out an SOS distress call to, not so much inside USDS itself but externally with contractors and different government partners. There’s a fair amount of backstabbing in the government, even in the civil service. Whenever you chose to reach out to a 3rd party to ask for help you were taking a risk that the person you reached out to would screw you over. Just because DC doesn’t have a palace doesn’t mean we don’t have palace intrigues.
 Anyway, one day this group of security researchers reached out to me and let me know that the agency they were working with was being actively targeted by domestic white supremacists. They had alerted the appropriate people and made recommendations but were getting the run around. The website the white supremacists were interested in targeting had  thousands of known security vulnerabilities but it also had a very specific launch date non-negotiable and written into law (this is a stupidly common thing in government). Leadership did not want to stop development work to fix security issues, but they also could not be seen to be ignoring security issues. Especially not thousands of them. So they would engage with the researchers, put on this big performance with meetings and field trips and PowerPoint decks, and then do nothing.
 And they were doing this on multiple fronts, slow walking both external consultants and government oversight authorities. It wasn’t a situation where we could report the problem up to one of the many organizations in charge of information security in the federal government. Everyone who could be called upon to apply pressure was already aware of the situation and applying maximum pressure.
 I suggested we let the FBI know and see if they could block the potential attackers for us without worrying about the website. No crime had yet been committed, so the FBI couldn’t technically do anything about the threats. But they do track this kind of information and perhaps what the security researchers had could support other investigations. It took me a couple of weeks to find the right contact and reach out. After establishing that he was on the relevant task force and that they did track this info and were interested in hearing about what we had found, I suggested we go grab coffee and fill in some of the details. I had provided a set of potential locations, including the agency cafeteria which was super nice.
 “Actually do you have an office or a conference room we can use?”
 Sure, I said. And this didn’t bother me because the phrase  let’s get coffee is always kind of figurative. To begin with, I don’t drink coffee.
 A huge part of my job at USDS was coffee dates. The way we fought the bureaucracy was to build networks of people whose goals were aligned and whose skill sets or resources were compatible. The first contact with a new node was always informal. I needed to size you up, determine your incentives, your tolerance for risk, how you defined success. But I also needed to establish trust. I never thought to handle this situation any differently just because the FBI was on the other end of it.
 Anyway, the agent comes up to my office, he closes the door and takes out his badge. “I have to show you this first.”
 This seemed strange to me because I did not actually have any reason to believe he wasn’t who he said he was, but certain jobs will make you paranoid so I shrugged it off.
 As we chatted, I noticed he was taking a lot of very thorough notes, which struck me as odd but — again — I dismissed. The conversation was friendly and productive. He set expectations appropriately. We agreed on how to follow up later. He stood up to leave, shook my hand, turned for the door, but stopped short as he touched the door knob.
 “Oh wait, I forgot something. Could you raise your right hand for a minute?”
  “Everything you told me today is true to the best of your knowledge?”
 I looked at my still raised right hand.   Oh…….. “This is one of those things where if it isn’t I go to jail for a million years, right?”
    Dazed, starting to appreciate that I might have accidentally bitten off way more than I could chew, I eventually wandered back to USDS HQ, which is a townhouse off of Lafayette Park just outside the White House. I ran into Matt Cutts in the foyer.
  “The strangest thing just happened,” I said before telling him the whole story. When I finished I realized that Matt was just  staring at me, completely silent. He stared at me like that for an uncomfortably long time.
      “I have two hundred employees, Marianne. It’s always you. No one else gets into these situations.  It’s always you. If someone had told me that one of my employees was hunting white supremacists with the FBI I wouldn’t have to ask who it was because, of course it’s you. It’s always you. Why is it always you?”
 People tend to think that the biggest deciding factor in their career is their talent or skill, but I don’t believe that. The way you process risk and handle fear has more impact on what kind of career you have than any other single factor. At USDS people saw me as something of a big game hunter. One of those few people always tied up on really critical national security type things, but I didn’t pursue those projects I just took opportunities that came across my desk and that other people were too scared to act on. With the FBI situation a large part of my ability to do that was rooted in ignorance. I didn’t stop to think that the FBI’s culture and process might assign a significance to my outreach that other organizations would not. But I also didn’t see myself as taking on this huge responsibility and all the corresponding risks. I was just there to connect two groups of people. To open the door for them so that they could get things done. I didn’t worry about whether I was qualified or capable of handling domestic white supremacists because I didn’t see that as my role. I trusted the researchers to be good at their jobs. I trusted the FBI to be good at their jobs.
 A lot of getting people to perform well as their manager is about anticipating their decision making, and a lot of that is about understanding two things:
  But it’s not just about managers considering their employees, surviving in an organization at any level involves anticipating the behavior of others. When people begin to take on leadership roles eventually they have to decide how they are going to handle fear. When you are the leader, you are the person who is held accountable for failure. The higher up you go, the more you are accountable for and the less you have control over it. Trust in you team is important, but your perception and acceptance of risk even more so. Leaders with a low tolerance for risk do not become leaders with a high tolerance for risk if you change their employees looking for trustworthy ones. Everybody wants to have this implicit , instinctual, don’t-need-to-think-about-it-and-never-doubt-it-for-a-second trust. That’s the gold standard. But most relationships of trust are formed because you just choose to set aside your doubts and trust, not because you have these great reservoirs of faith.
 Leaders as they develop tend to break in one of two directions on risk. There are the leaders that — like samurai — embrace death. They see their role as protecting and empowering the people below them who do the real work and it’s an honor to serve the team’s interests even if things do not go well for them personally. In the best organizations the leader’s manager also does not fear death and they have the same safety as they grant to their people.
 But some people don’t process risk in that way. Those leaders get drawn into trying to outrun death, which is as impossible metaphorically as it would be literally. You can’t avoid taking responsibility forever. Eventually there will be some disappointment or mistake that you will be called on to answer for.
 The leaders who have a low tolerance for risk — and I say leaders instead of  managers because on engineering teams these concepts also apply to Staff and Principal engineers — will respond to risk by either avoiding it outright or figuring out how to spin the situation so that it is Not Their Fault(TM)
 These leaders ultimately become bureaucrats. The perfect job is the job with the glamorous title and the big paycheck where no decisions and no risks need to be taken. These leaders end up in startups more often than you might expect. That’s because continuing down a career where you’re running from death all the time does real damage to you psychologically. It’s important to understand that even though it seems like these people don’t care they are still human and they still have the same basic needs and feelings as anyone else. Fleeing death doesn’t just hurt the people they throw under the bus, it also hurts them. They want to be respected by their employees and often know they are not. They want to be admired and often know they are not. They also feel guilty screwing people over to survive.
 When you are in a work situation where you are respected and admired, that is often its own reward. If you don’t have that, the components of your job that give you a sense of value and satisfaction are all connected directly to the career ladder. What is your title? What is the prestige value of your projects? How many people report up to you? How many people report up to them? How much are you getting paid? How much equity do you have? All that shit becomes very important because it is often all you have. People who flee death are in a state of constant anxiety. It’s like the worst form of impostor syndrome. They self soothe by climbing the career ladder. So even though a startup may seem like a poor fit if you don’t want to take on responsibility and just want to rubber stamp things all day, those opportunities are very attractive to leaders who fear death because the prestige of them is a substitute for the satisfaction of doing a good job.
 And once people start working that way it becomes a cycle that escalates. No one is born knowing how to run a 1,000 person engineering organization. People need time to build skills before they are ready for a promotion. If your actual job triggers enough fear and anxiety for you to betray the trust of your closest colleagues, you won’t do better one level up. The further beyond your area of competence you get, the worse the stress gets, the faster you need to run to escape death.
 Eventually you end up spending all your time trying to look busy, avoid responsibility and save face by scapegoating others. Meanwhile leaders who don’t fear death tend to have slower career progressions because they take more satisfaction from their actual jobs and focus on building skills that will make them better. The end result of this is that it often feels like everyone at the top is maliciously incompetent.
title: 为什么这个白痴运行我的工程组织？
title_en: Why Is This Idiot Running My Engineering Org?

note:
有些人对笔记本电脑上的摄像头或他们多年前购买的旧摄像头不满意。
 我的兄弟决定他要尝试流式传输。有些人可以做某事，而另一些人则想事先研究所有点点滴滴。我们的聊天记录的长度与我所有其他聊天记录的总长度相当，因此我也学到了很多。工艺上充满了小细节和不便。
 他发现基本上没有好的网络摄像头。很多时候，图像质量为meh，而4k的罕见存在通常不优于1080p，因为分辨率不是质量。一些相机显然在其驱动程序中确实从1080p升级到4k，这表现为质量差和CPU负载高。他还拥有非常出色的相机Fujifilm X-T1。连接到USB端口时，不能用作网络摄像头。通常可以购买HDMI到USB的转换器，但是他的相机无法将实时流输出到HDMI，只能输出录制的视频。
 因此，我们有了一个主意-也许可以创建一个出色的网络摄像头。有人会在乎吗？嗯，现在快到2021年，流媒体仍在增长，大流行病日益猖and，视频会议无处不在。人们愿意花一些钱，正如YouTube上无数关于该问题的视频所表明的那样。
  在我看来，一个好的网络摄像头是一种能够提供良好音质的设备，至少可以与最近的MacBook相提并论，并且可以与顶级智能手机（如iPhone 11/12，Huawei P40 Pro等）相提并论。具有良好的图像质量，那么我们可能可以使用便宜且易于使用的Smartphoneparts制造网络摄像头。它能有多难？
 如果您至少阅读了几篇有关视频制作的文章，您将知道，从技术角度来看，制作高质量的内容需要按照以下特定顺序进行三件事：
    如果您拥有最新的MacBook，它的三麦克风阵列可提供不错的音质。我还没有研究过，对其他笔记本也不是很了解。音频质量。如果您有一台台式PC，则无论如何都必须有一个外部麦克风。 
如果网络摄像机的音频质量低劣，则必须购买其他齿轮。当我研究此主题时，最近发布的一个建议零售价为$ 180的网络摄像头（可从2020年10月开始使用）发出的声音好像是一个人坐在一个大塑料桶中并盖上了盖子。他们的声音被许多共鸣的嗡嗡声掩盖住了。十年前，这种质量的水平是不可接受的，如何在2020年生产出这种齿轮是我的误解。
 售价50美元以上的USB麦克风已经足够好了，您也可以花100美元购买一台出色的XLR麦克风，但是您需要一个带有phantompower电源的外部声卡。一个体面的人可以轻松地花费您200美元。
 使用几乎所有这些选项，您都将必须安装和配置一些软件，该软件将提供声级压缩，噪声抑制（因为您的笔记本风扇旋转得像地狱一样），并且在此过程中不会产生咔嗒声。
 我希望我的网络摄像头能够自己执行此操作，而不会发出嘎嘎声，回声最小化等。
  使画面更出色的许多原因都来自以正确的方式照明场景。
 最简单的事情是坐在房间里，这样窗户就在您的面前，而太阳就不会从肩膀后面照进镜头。
 网络摄像头本身无法提供良好的照明功能，因此我不会在这里花费很多时间。 Razer有一个带有内置LED圈的网络摄像头，但它一定很小，总是从网络摄像头的方向照亮，并且大多是a头。 
我们可以在这里依靠好的传感器。如果它提供了良好的动态范围，HDR功能和良好的弱光性能，则墙上的一小片阳光不会将其他所有物体变黑，或者光线不足的房间也不会变成带有噪点的灰色嘈杂画面。黯淡的轮廓。
  由于盖子非常薄，因此无法将优质的镜头和图像传感器装入笔记本计算机的盖子。它们通常合起来大约4毫米厚，并且没有逃避物理现象。当然，可以将网络摄像头放到盖子下面较厚的部分，但是这样对于用宽大的鼻孔向同事表达愤怒非常有用。
 也许将来有些疯狂的工程师会开发一系列带有小透镜的小型传感器，并以某种方式将所有这些结合到一个良好的图像质量中，但是我们还没有这项技术。任何配备了优质相机的智能手机，其镜头中丑陋突出的镜头都是手机最厚的部分。
 因此，如果您想要的东西比笔记本电脑的摄像头更好，则可以购买Logitech的摄像头，其图像质量可以追溯到2012年。它仍然比笔记本电脑更好，但与当前的智能手机相比还差得远。
 如果您还要更好呢？下一个选项是在手机上安装应用程序并将其用作网络摄像头。这样可以提供很好的图像质量，但是非常不方便，在某些应用程序中根本无法使用。出于某种原因，选择视频源时，并非总是将其作为选项显示在应用程序中。
 我不想不断地为手机安装，拆卸，充电并调整小三脚架的位置。下一个选择是什么？
 下一个选择是只购买带有4/3英寸肥大甚至APS-C传感器的无反光镜相机。新的将花费$ 500 +（您可以购买二手的checheaper）。您还需要一些装备来将相机固定在适当的位置，因为它不会像普通的网络摄像头那样恰好位于屏幕顶部。您还需要一个HDMI到USB的视频采集卡，并且需要确保购买的摄像机可以在该HDMI上实时输出全分辨率。另外，还要确保可以关闭所有屏幕信息，例如1 / 100s F3.5 ISO 800，这在您通过取景器拍摄照片和视频时很有用，而在您使用取景器拍摄照片和视频时不会有用。重新参加视频会议。该功能称为“清洁HDMI”。 
当然，您将对整个程序有很多控制-设置光圈膜，获得良好的散景，设置ISO，设置白平衡等。您实际上需要吗？好吧，你们当中有些人这样做，而很多人却没有。我从16岁起就学到了所有这些东西，但现在我对自己的手机如何选择所有东西感到非常满意，因为它们可以拍出精美的照片。
 所有这些听起来很复杂，不是吗？现在需要大量时间来研究您的需求，如何进行设置以及大量金钱。
  因此，在一般的100-200美元的网络摄像头与配备无反光镜相机，外部麦克风和照明面板的功能齐全的摄像头之间存在市场缺口，如果您愿意的话，这将花费近一两个镜头。
 我认为创建一个“高档”是个好主意。售价在250-300美元之间的网络摄像头，它将提供与当前智能手​​机相同的良好音频和视频质量，实际上使用的是相同的部件。随着智能手机的生产量已达数千万，所有相关部件都应便宜且易于获得。
 我迅速联系了一些可以帮助我完成该项目的人员-固件工程师，电气工程师和一家从事机械设计的公司。
 良好的视频直播和视频会议需要哪些功能？音频：良好的零件（麦克风和ADC），良好的机械设计和组装（震颤会嘎嘎作响）以及一些后处理。我们当然希望波束形成功能能够将焦点集中在网络摄像头前面的人上，因此我们将需要多个麦克风，也许最多需要4个。这些部件很容易获得，有很多库存，这很重要。没问题。
 即使我认为不需要4k，我也可以肯定高端网络摄像头确实是4k的要求，因为要出售只能支持1080p的昂贵网络摄像头真的很难。 
在芯片上进行神经网络推理也非常好，因此我们可以直接在网络摄像头上运行一些AI东西，而不会打扰可能忙于其他事情的用户计算机。
 我很容易地找到了三块钱的iPhone 11 Pro组件，价格为五十美元，两块钱来自华为P40 Pro，价格为二十五美元，一束来自P40 Lite，价格为十美元。带有镜头，是零售购买的。批量购买生产我自己的网络摄像头是否意味着要花花生（传感器不到5美元）？我不能只是去买几千个这样的传感器。我很确定我可以购买成千上万的手机替换零件，但是传感器本身并不是免费出售的。买些随机的东西很容易传感器已经存在多年了，并且肯定具有＆＃34; meh＆＃34;的质量达到当今的标准，但我们需要一个良好的产品，同时具有良好的动态范围，HDR功能和低光性能。
 我写信给几家供应商，问他们价格和条件是什么，基本上他们的回答要么是沉默，要么是“如果您每年要购买数十万，我们将出售它”。
 我已经找到了合适的片上系统，并且一样—如果我们想要硬件4k编码器，对不起，这是演示部分，我们没有。 ＃39不这样做，或者我们的支持能力有限，因此我们只能在您要购买数十万，也许您想要从我们的合作伙伴那里购买一些SoM的情况下才能出售？实际上，即使购买非常少的模块系统，也是可能的，但是这将是非常昂贵的，并且对于原型来说将是一个好选择，但是在生产中，这将超出我们的预算。模块化系统的成本几乎与网络摄像机本身相同。
 哦，交货时间（从订购到发货）系统地超过16周。表示他们没有库存，将在半导体代工厂下订单，生产，包装并发送给客户。迅速地。
 这意味着我不能只建立五百个网络摄像头，看看它们的销售情况如何，还可以建立几千个，等等。
 还有一个塑料注射步骤，需要对制造模具进行前期投资，当然还需要设计和工程成本。全部加起来是数万美元。我愿意冒险，并且可以肯定的是，在最坏的情况下，这只是我积累硬件产品经验的成本。 
但是，如果我必须承诺制造十万个或更多的设备，这意味着我将需要投入数百万美元（我没有）来制造大量我不知道如何出售的设备。
 我考虑了几天，并且明白从一开始我就从错误的角度解决了这个问题。
 实际的问题不是如何构建好的网络摄像机。真正的问题是如何批量出售！我有可能每年出售成千上万的昂贵网络摄像头吗？如果我知道我将出售它，我可以为此寻找投资。
 让我们在这里描述我的情况。我是SaaS的prophy.science的联合创始人，并且全职从事这项工作。我可能可以将20％的时间花在其他事情上，但是肯定不想完全放弃它。我还有两个漂亮的小孩子，我真的不想搬到深圳在那里组织大型制作。
  好的，目前的市场规模是多少？幸运的是，市场上有上市公司，所以我们可以看看他们的报告，看看是否有有意义的事情。罗技表示，他们从PC网络摄像头的销售中获得约1.3亿美元的收入。从长远来看，我认为他们是市场上最大的参与者，并且每年售出几百万个网络摄像头。那...不多吗？
 这意味着我必须从一开始就出售Logitech出货量的10％。
  它在市场上已经建立了稳固的地位，所有的分销渠道到位，品牌知名度很高。我来了，我对如何销售硬件产品一无所知，而且似乎没有办法进入市场并扩大规模。 
好吧，也许有，但我看不到。也许我可以通过深入研究并专心研究这个问题多年来解决这个问题，但是现在看来情况要复杂得多。
 我还要指出，这个市场基本上是无法防御的。当他们看到有一个好的利基市场时，是什么阻止了做同样事情的球员？他们只是去找相同的供应商，获得相同的零件/价格，并使用他们已建立的分销渠道粉碎一家初创公司。
 我记得很多采访中的一个主语，如果我知道这有多难，我就不会做。我们在这里，知道将要付出多大的努力，而不是做。
 我只是回到我的SaaS上，这更具防御性，非常感谢。
 我的兄弟实际上开始直播了，所以您可以去看他用ClojureScript和React编写蛇游戏。
 回答标题中的问题：您不能购买优质的网络摄像头，因为现有玩家似乎并不认为这是一个很好的机会，而对于新手来说，很难进入市场。
 我们也很幸运，相机制造商在比较便宜的相机中添加了干净的实时HDMI，因此我们不需要花费$ 3000 +来进行全部设置。
note_en:
Some people are not satisfied with a webcam from their notebook or an old webcamthey bought many years ago.
 My brother decided he wants to try streaming. Some people can just do a thingand other people want to research all the bits and pieces beforehand. Our chathistory is comparable in length to all my other chats combined, so I learned alot of it as well. The craft is full with small details and inconveniences.
 He found that there are basically no good webcams. A lot of the time an imagequality is meh, and the rare presence of 4k is usually not better than 1080p,because resolution is not quality. Some camera apparently does upscaling from1080p to 4k in its driver, indicated by poor quality and high CPU load. He alsohas a really good camera, Fujifilm X-T1. It can't be used as a webcam whenconnected to a USB port. Usually it's possible to buy an HDMI-to-USB converter,but his camera can't output a live stream into HDMI, only a recorded video.
 So we had an idea — maybe it is possible to create a good webcamourselves. Would anybody care? Well, it’s almost 2021 now, streaming is still onthe rise, pandemic is going strong and video meetings are everywhere. People arewilling to spend some money, as indicated by countless videos on YouTuberegarding the problem.
  A good webcam, in my opinion, is a device that provides a decent sound quality,at least on par with recent MacBooks, and an image quality on par with topsmartphones, such as iPhone 11/12, Huawei P40 Pro, etc. As smartphones have agood image quality, then we could probably manufacture a webcam from smartphoneparts that are cheap and easily available. How hard can it be?
 If you’ve read at least a couple of articles on the topic of video production,you will know that to make quality content from the technical standpoint youneed three things, in that particular order:
    If you have a recent MacBook, it provides a decent sound quality with itsthree-mic array. I haven't researched it and don’t know much about othernotebooks' audio quality. If you have a desktop PC, you have to have someexternal mic anyway.
 If the web camera has shitty audio quality, you’ll have to buy additionalgear. When I researched the topic, one of the recently released webcams(available from October 2020) with RRP of $180 was producing a sound as if aperson sits in a big plastic barrel with a closed lid. Their voice was verymuffled with lots of resonant rattles. That level of quality wasn’t acceptable10 years ago, how this piece of gear could have been produced in 2020 escapes myunderstanding.
 There are many good enough USB mics for $50+, you also can buy a great XLRmicrophone for $100, but then you’ll need an external sound card with phantompower supply. A decent one can easily cost you $200.
 With almost any of these options you’ll have to install and configure somesoftware that will provide sound level compression, noise suppression (becauseyour notebook fans are spinning like hell) and won’t produce clicks in theprocess.
 I would like my webcam to do this by itself, no rattling, minimized echo, etc.
  A lot of what makes a picture great comes from lighting the scene in a correct way.
 The simplest thing is to sit in a room so the window is in front of you and theSun isn’t shining into a lens from behind your shoulder.
 Webcam cannot provide decent lighting capabilities by itself, so I won’t spendmuch time here. There is a  webcam byRazerwith a built-in LED circle, but it’s necessarily small, always shines from awebcam direction and is mostly a gimmick.
 We can rely somewhat on the good sensor here. If it provides good dynamic range,HDR capabilities and good low-light performance, then a patch of sunlight on thewall won't turn everything else black, or a poorly lit room won't turn into agray noisy picture with a bleak silhouette.
  It’s impossible to get a good lens and image sensor into a notebook lid, becauselids are very thin. They’re often something like 4 mm thick all together, andthere is no escaping physics. Of course, it's possible to put a webcam into athick part below the lid, but then it'll be useful for expressing anger at ourcolleagues with wide-open nostrils and not much else.
 Maybe some crazy engineer in the future will develop an array of small sensorswith an array of tiny lenses and somehow combine all that into a good imagequality, but we don't have this tech yet. Any smartphone with a good camera hasthat camera with an ugly protruding lens as a thickest part of the phone.
 So, if you want something better than a notebook's webcam, you can get someLogitech webcam with an image quality going back to 2012. It’s still better thana laptop, but nowhere close to current smartphones.
 What if you want better still? The next option is to install an app on the phoneand use it as a webcam. This gives a very good image quality, but it’s veryinconvenient and in some applications doesn’t work at all. For some reason itisn’t always presented in applications as an option when choosing a videosource.
 I don’t want to constantly attach, detach, charge my phone and tune the position ofa small tripod. What’s the next option?
 The next option is to just buy a mirrorless photo-camera with a big fat 4/3” oreven APS-C sensor. It will cost $500+ for a new one (you can buy used forcheaper). You will also need some gear to hold the camera in place, as it won’tjust rest on the top of the screen like a regular webcam. You also need anHDMI-to-USB video grabber, and you need to be sure that the camera you buy canoutput full resolution live onto that HDMI. Also make sure that it's possible toturn off all on-screen info like  1/100s F3.5 ISO 800, which is useful whenyou're shooting photos and videos through viewfinder, but not so much whenyou're in a video meeting. This feature is called "Clean HDMI".
 Of course, you will have a lot of control over the whole stuff — set adiaphragm, get good bokeh, set ISO, set white balance, etc. Do you actually needit? Well, some of you do, and a lot of you do not. I learned all that stuff whenI was 16, but now I’m pretty happy with how my phone chooses everything for meand just does great photos.
 All that sounds pretty involved, isn’t it? It is a lot of time to research whatyou need, how to set it all up, and a lot of money too.
  So there is a market gap between so-so webcams for $100-200 and a full-blownsetup with a mirrorless camera, an external mic and lighting panels that willcost almost a grand or two, if you're so inclined.
 I thought that it is a good idea to create an "upmarket" webcam for $250-300that will provide good audio and video quality, on par with current smartphones,actually using the same parts. As smartphones are made in tens of millions, allrelevant parts should be cheap and easily obtainable.
 I quickly contacted some people who could help me do the project — firmwareengineer, electric engineer and a company that does mechanical design.
 What features are needed for a good webcam for streamers and video meetings?Audio: good parts (mics and ADC), good mechanical design and assembly (sonothing would rattle), and some post-processing. We certainly want somebeam-forming capabilities to be able to focus on the person in front of thewebcam, so we will need more than one microphone, maybe up to 4. These parts areeasy to obtain, there are many in stock, it's not a problem.
 Even if I think that 4k is not needed, I’m pretty sure it’s really a requirementto have in an upmarket webcam, as it will be really hard to sell a pricey webcamthat can only do 1080p.
 Also would be very nice to have some neural network inference on the chip, so wecan run some AI stuff directly on the webcam without bothering a user’s computerthat can be busy with other things.
 I easily found a three-piece assembly for the iPhone 11 Pro for fifty bucks,two-piece from a Huawei P40 Pro for twenty five and a one-piece from P40 Lite forten dollars. That’s with a lens, and bought in retail. Does it mean it costspeanuts (less than $5 for a sensor) to buy in bulk to produce my own webcam?Well... Maybe. I can’t just go and buy a couple thousands of these sensors. I’mpretty sure I can buy a couple thousands of replacement parts for phones, butsensors themselves are not sold freely. It's easy to buy some "random" sensorthat was around for years and most certainly has "meh" quality by today'sstandards, but we need a good one, with good dynamic range, HDR capabilities andlow-light performance.
 I wrote to several vendors asking them what are the prices and conditions, andbasically their answer is either silence or “we’ll sell it if you will buyhundreds of thousands per year”.
 I've found suitable System-on-Chips, and it's the same — if we want to havehardware 4k encoder, it's either "sorry, it was a demo part and we don't do it",or "we have limited support capabilities, so we can only sell if you will buyhundreds of thousands, maybe you want some SoMs from our partners?". It isactually possible to buy System-on-Modules even in very small quantities, butit's going to be pretty expensive and will be good for a prototype, but forproduction it will blow past our budget. System-on-Module will cost almost thesame as the web camera itself.
 Oh, and a lead time (from order to shipment) is systematically 16+ weeks. Itmeans they have no stock and will place an order at semiconductor foundry, haveit produced, packaged and sent to customer. Swiftly.
 It means I cannot just build five hundred webcams, see how good are they sellingand build a couple thousand, and so on.
 There is also a plastic injection step that requires upfront investment tomanufacture molds, and of course design and engineering costs. All combined itwas tens of thousands of dollars. I was willing to risk it and was pretty surethat in the worst case scenario it would be just a cost of my experiencebuilding hardware product.
 But if I have to commit to a hundred thousand units or more, it means I wouldneed to pour millions of dollars (that I don’t have) to manufacture an obsceneamount of devices that I don’t know how to sell.
 I thought about it for a couple of days and understood that I approached theproblem from the wrong angle at the beginning.
 The actual problem is not how to build a good web camera. The real question ishow to sell it in quantity! Is it possible for me to sell hundreds of thousandsof pricey webcams per year? If I know I will sell it, I can find an investmentfor that.
 Let's describe my situation here. I’m a co-founder at prophy.science which is a SaaS, and I work on itfull-time. I can probably spend 20% of my time on something else, but Icertainly don’t want to abandon it completely. I also have two small beautifulkids, and I really don’t want to move to Shenzhen to organize a big productionthere.
  Ok, what’s the current size of the market? Luckily, there are public companiesin the market, so we can go look at their reports to see if there is anythingmeaningful. And there is, Logitech says they have about $130M in revenue fromsales of PC webcams. I think they’re the biggest player in the market by a longshot, and they sell something like a few millions of webcams ayear. That’s... not much?
 It means that I have to sell like 10% of the Logitech shipment size, right fromthe start.
  It’s an established player in the market with all the distribution in place,with a very recognizable brand, and here am I, knowing nothing about how to sellhardware products, and there is seemingly no way to slither into the market andscale.
 Well, maybe there is, but I don’t see it. Maybe I could pull it off by divingdeep down and going full-time on the problem for years, but it seems much morerisky now.
 Also I should mention that this market is basically indefensible. Whatprevents established players from doing pretty much the same, when they will seethat there is a good niche? They just go to the same suppliers, get same orbetter parts/prices and crush a startup using their established distributionchannels.
 I remember a staple phrase from a lot of interviews, something along the linesof "If I knew how hard it would be, I wouldn't do it". Here we are, knowing howhard it will be, and not doing it.
 I'm just going back to my SaaS, which is much more defensible, thank you verymuch.
 My brother actually  startedstreaming,so you can go watch him code a  snake game in ClojureScript andReact.
 Answering the question in title: you can't buy a good webcam because existingplayers seemingly don't think it's a good opportunity, and for newcomers themarket is hard to enter.
 We're also lucky that camera manufacturers added clean live HDMI intocomparatively cheap cameras, so we don't need to spend $3000+ to set it all up.
title: 为什么不能购买优质的网络摄像头？
title_en: Why can’t you buy a good webcam?

最近公布的COVID-19刺激法案包含一系列措施，这些措施不仅与冠状病毒无关，而且可能因涉嫌侵犯版权而导致视频共享服务的创建者和提供者被判入狱并处以巨额罚款。
 该法案中与版权有关的有争议的修正案列在“标题II-知识产权”下。
 这些修订的第一个是“ 2319C。非法数字传输服务”将使一个人“故意，出于商业利益或私人财务利益的目的”成为非法，向公众提供“主要为公共目的设计或提供”的“数字传输服务”未经版权拥有者或法律的授权，执行受标题17保护的作品。”
 现有的版权法包含一项合理使用的条款，该条款允许在某些情况下未经版权所有者许可而使用受版权保护的材料（例如，如果使用被认为是“具有转化性的”，因为它为原始作品增加了新的表达或含义） ）。
 但是，由于创作者必须在法庭上证明合理使用，因此版权所有者经常滥用该系统，并使用版权声明删除符合合理使用标准的内容。
 这项“非法数字传输服务”修正案可能会为针对视频共享平台提供商的类似类型的滥用敞开大门，并使他们面临监禁的威胁，除非他们删除符合合理使用标准但具有被可疑的版权声明所打击。
 另一个有争议的修正案是纳入了“ 2020年小额索赔执行法中的版权替代法”（CASE法）。该修正案提议在美国版权局内引入版权主张委员会（CCB），以使版权所有者更容易提出版权主张并收取损害赔偿。该法案还建议对每宗案件处以最高30,000美元的罚款。 
《反腐败法》于去年首次提出，并遭到了严厉批评。数字版权组织电子前沿基金会（EFF）警告说，该法案几乎使那些被指控侵犯版权的人无法上诉，而且该法案可能会使常规的互联网用户破产。
 数字版权组织“为未来而战”猛烈抨击将CASE Act纳入此COVID-19刺激法案中：
 “这是残暴的。我们正面临着大规模的迁离危机，大流行使数百万人失业，但是国会领导人只能召集600美元的刺激性检查以减轻COVID的负担，却设法向迪士尼等内容公司补习？ 《案例分析法》是一部非常成文的法律，它将对普通的互联网用户每天的在线活动处以巨额罚款。议员将这些规定纳入必不可少的支出法案中，这是荒谬的。”
 为未来而战还呼吁众议院和参议院领导人“从持续决议中删除版权条款，并按常规顺序进行调整，以使我们可以就适当的平衡问题进行透明，公开的辩论”，并敦促立法者“建立一个公平的制度来保护人权并确保艺术家得到合理的补偿，而不是通过拙劣的立法来捣乱，因为立法可能会惩罚普通的互联网用户从事日常活动，如共享模因和在线下载图像。
note_en:
The recently unveiled COVID-19 stimulus bill contains a series of measures that not only have nothing to do with the coronavirus but could lead to prison time and huge fines for creators and providers of video sharing services that are accused of copyright infringement.
 The controversial copyright-related amendments to  the bill are listed under “TITLE II—INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY.”
 The first of these amendments is “2319C. Illicit digital transmission services” which will make it illegal for a person to “willfully, and for purposes of commercial advantage or private financial gain” provide a “digital transmission service” to the public that is “primarily designed or provided for the purpose of publicly performing works protected under title 17 without the authority of the copyright owner or the law.”
 Existing copyright law contains a provision for fair use that allows copyrighted material to be used without permission from the copyright holder in certain circumstances (for example, if the use is deemed to be “transformative” because it adds new expression or meaning to the original work).
 However, because creators have to prove fair use in court, copyright holders often abuse the system and use copyright claims to take down content that meets the criteria for fair use.
 This “Illicit digital transmission services” amendment could open the door for similar types of abuse that are targeted at the providers of video-sharing platforms and leave them facing the threat of prison time unless they remove content that meets the criteria for fair use but has been hit with questionable copyright claims.
 Another controversial amendment is the inclusion of the “Copyright Alternative in Small-Claims Enforcement Act of 2020” ( the CASE Act). This amendment proposes the introduction of a Copyright Claims Board (CCB) within the US Copyright Office which makes it easier for copyright holders to file copyright claims and collect damages. The bill also proposes fines of up to $30,000 per case.
 The CASE Act was first proposed last year and has faced heavy criticism. Digital rights groups the Electronic Frontier Foundation (EFF) has warned that the bill makes it almost impossible for those accused of copyright violations to appeal and that it could bankrupt regular internet users.
 Digital rights group Fight for the Future  slammed the inclusion of the CASE Act in this COVID-19 stimulus bill:
 “This is atrocious. We’re facing a massive eviction crisis and millions are unemployed due to the pandemic, but Congressional leaders could only muster $600 stimulus checks for COVID relief, but managed to cram in handouts for content companies like Disney? The CASE Act is a terribly written law that will threaten ordinary Internet users with huge fines for everyday online activity. It’s absurd that lawmakers included these provisions in a must-pass spending bill.”
 Fight for the Future also called House and Senate leaders to “remove the copyright provisions from the Continuing Resolution and move them through regular order so we can have transparent and open debate about the right balance” and urged lawmakers to “create a fair system that protects human rights and ensures artists are fairly compensated instead of ramming through poorly crafted legislation that could punish ordinary Internet users for engaging in everyday activities like sharing memes and downloading images online.”
